data.body:
PASS 1 review — [T3] Integration feasibility (overlay): PASS, no findings.

The only external integration this plan asserts is **ruff** (enable BLE001 + scoped T201, add a `per-file-ignores` allowlist). This is an internal, already-used integration — the overlay leans toward not-applicable — but I verified every asserted capability against the codebase and all check out:

- (a) technical_feasibility — ACHIEVABLE, no capability gap. ruff is already pinned (`ruff==0.15.19`, pyproject.toml:106) and wired through `make lint` + CI (Makefile:28; .github/workflows/test.yml:50). `[tool.ruff.lint]` already exists with `select = ["E","F","W","I","UP","B"]` (pyproject.toml:178-179); adding `BLE001`/`T201` to `select` plus a `per-file-ignores` table is standard, well-supported ruff config.
- (b) named rules/config keys exist — MATCH. `BLE001` and `T201` are real ruff codes already recognized in-tree (95+ `# noqa: BLE001` and 42 `# noqa: T201` annotations present and effective). `per-file-ignores` is a documented ruff config key. The shrinking-allowlist exemplar `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` exists (one-path-per-line, lines 11-17) and matches the pattern the plan clones. The plan's load-bearing claim that "all 42 existing `# noqa: T201` sites live [in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/`], NOT in the CLI" is verified EXACTLY — every T201 hit is under `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/`; none in `_cli`/`_commands`/`plan_review`.
- (c) auth/HTTPS preconditions — correctly N/A (local linter, no network/credentials).
- (d) no critical capability gap → no SPIKE required.

Affirmations (T3): integration achievable as described; named rule codes + `per-file-ignores` key verified present (MATCH); auth/HTTPS correctly not-applicable; no capability gap routing to a SPIKE.

(Scope note, not a T3 finding and already raised by the G1G2/G4 passes: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` has zero real `print(`/`file=sys.stderr` sites, so What-bullet 2 / AC #2's print-migration step has no in-package target. Out of scope for T3 — flagged only for cross-reference.)
